zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley於1327年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser41歲，母親Eleanor de Clare35歲。她於1348年8月與Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1389年7月13日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年62歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 波托爾福 阿爾德斯蓋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley于1327年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser41岁，母亲Eleanor de Clare35岁。她于1348年8月与Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1389年7月13日在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年62岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦 聖 波托爾福 阿爾德斯蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley was born in 1327, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, was 41 and her mother, Eleanor de Clare, was 35. She married Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley in August 1348, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 13 July 1389, in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley est née en 1327, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Clare, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley en août 1348, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 13 juillet 1389, à Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middlesex, Angleterre.

Sie hat im August 1348 in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. Juli 1389 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley è nata il nel 1327 nel London, Middlesex, England, sua padre, Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Eleanor de Clare, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley nel agosto 1348, nel Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 13 luglio 1389, nel Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeleyが1327年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenserは41歳，母Eleanor de Clareは35歳であった。1348年8月にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーでMaurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1389年7月13日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアソルンブリーで62歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン サイント ボトルフ アルダースガトにて埋葬された。

ko.London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middles: 영국 미들레세스 런던 세인트 벗프 알더스가트
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley(이)가 1327년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor de Clare(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1348년 8월 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1389년 7월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 토른버이에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던 세인트 벗프 알더스가트에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley em agosto de 1348, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middles: лондон сеинт ботолф алдирсгэит, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth le Despenser Baroness Berkeley родилась в 1327 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Clare, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley август 1348 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 13 июля 1389 г. в местности таурнбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности лондон сеинт ботолф алдирсгэит, миддлесекс, Англия.

Se casó con Maurice de Berkeley 4th Baron Berkeley en agosto de 1348, en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 13 de julio de 1389, en Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en London Saint Botolph Aldersgate, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
